extra.reasoning: The property requires NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). rec_drug_a is set true by r1 when (elderly AND g6pd_deficiency); rec_drug_b is set true by r3 when elderly. These two rules write to DIFFERENT outputs, so the per-output priority/tie-breaking conflict resolution never pits them against each other — each wins on its own output independently. With elderly=true and g6pd_deficiency=true, r1 fires (rec_drug_a:=true) and r3 fires (rec_drug_b:=true) simultaneously, so rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b is true. qt_prolongation is irrelevant (set false here; r2 only affects rec_drug_c). This input is reachable and makes both drug a and drug b jointly recommended, violating mutual exclusion.
